reasoning: The most recent value of the 1-month CMT yield is securely anchored at 3.72 following the late January FOMC decision. However, in early January it hit 3.78 under the same target range, so a reading >3.72 does not strictly require a Fed rate hike. The FRED statistical tool directly calculated a 64% probability of the yield exceeding 3.72% by February 22 based on the recent linearly predicted trendline and historical seasonality, which I have used as my primary anchor. The market consensus predicts the Fed will hold the 3.5%-3.75% range through March 17 as inflation is still elevated. The Treasury also noted it would support benchmark bill sizes into mid-March. Because of the prolonged time horizon towards March 17, and standard forecasting conventions, uncertainty inherently increases, meaning my estimate for the later date is pulled closer toward a 50% baseline.
